data.description:
## Why

A core driver of the visual-editing goal is letting developers ADD / REMOVE individual criteria
(prompts) and see/adjust their inclusion conditions — i.e. the overlay predicate — and the batch
grouping. The visual editor must therefore understand the two NEW constructs this epic adds. Scope
is bounded to the new components, not a general canvas.

## What

Extend the workflow visual editor (see docs/workflow-editor.md) to render and edit the overlay
`when`/`enabled` predicate on a prompt step and the `batch` step (its group/budget/ladder params),
including ADD/REMOVE of a conditionally-included prompt. Preserve the engine invariants: clean git
diffs + schema-validated text round-trip (the e6d9 editor invariant). The demo workflow from the
sibling story is the refinement sample.

## Scope

- The workflow editor surface (the bpmn-js / editor assets + the Python render/round-trip; see
  docs/workflow-editor.md and tests/e2e/test_editor_*).
- Render + edit the overlay predicate and the `batch` step; add/remove a conditionally-included prompt.
- Round-trip must keep clean git diffs + schema validation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The editor renders a prompt step's overlay `when`/`enabled` predicate and the `batch` step
      (group/budget/ladder), and supports ADD/REMOVE of a conditionally-included prompt.
- [ ] Editor round-trip preserves clean git diffs + schema-validated text (the e6d9 invariant).
- [ ] An editor test (tests/e2e or equivalent) covers rendering/editing the two new constructs.
- [ ] make lint + the relevant editor/workflow tests pass.
data.title: Visual editing for the new constructs (overlay predicate + batch step) in the workflow editor
